title: Implementing Regulation of the Saudi Personal Data Protection Law
title_ar: اللائحة التنفيذية لنظام حماية البيانات الشخصية
official_title: Implementing Regulation of the Personal Data Protection Law
official_title_ar: اللائحة التنفيذية لنظام حماية البيانات الشخصية
citation: Implementing Regulation of the Personal Data Protection Law, issued by SDAIA and published in the Official Gazette on 7 September 2023
summary: The operational layer of the PDPL, issued by SDAIA one week before the law entered into force. It details consent mechanics, data subject request handling, breach notification, records of processing, data protection officer appointment triggers, and impact assessments.
impact_note: This is where PDPL compliance becomes concrete. The regulation sets the 72 hour breach notification window to SDAIA, defines when a data protection officer must be appointed, prescribes the content of records of processing activities and privacy notices, and details legitimate interest assessments. Compliance teams should build their PDPL controls directly against this text rather than the law alone, and track SDAIA's subsequent amendments and guidance, which refine these requirements.

obligations.description: Notify SDAIA of personal data breaches that harm personal data or data subjects
Appoint a data protection officer where the regulation's triggers are met
Maintain records of processing activities and make privacy notices available before collection
Conduct impact assessments for processing likely to cause high risk to data subjects

entity_types: foreign entities processing data of Saudi residents
applicability.penalties: Violations are sanctioned under the PDPL penalty framework
applicability.grace_period: Aligned with the PDPL transition that ended 14 September 2024

notes: SDAIA amended this regulation in September 2024 alongside the transfer rules. The amendment is not yet recorded as a separate entry; add it during the research build out and move this record's lifecycle to amended.
